sources.notes: Statement and constraints: count valid bounded arrays b with 0 <= b_i <= n modulo 1e9+7.
Luogu mirror used to cross-check statement and constraints.
Used to confirm the shared feasibility test with E1 and the O(n) product formula.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not used as evidence in this review.

annotation.summary: Count how many bounded arrays b produce the given sequence of prefix kth-mex values.
annotation.constraints: t <= 1e4, sum n <= 2e5, and b_i is restricted to [0,n], allowing a linear product formula modulo 1e9+7.
annotation.core_idea: The same conditions from E1 are necessary and sufficient: a_i must be nonincreasing and n - i - 1 <= a_i <= n. Valid constructions become independent local choices. If a_i equals the previous target, the next value must consume one still-missing non-target value below a_i, giving a_i - (n - i - 1) choices. If the target starts a lower level, there are i + 1 bounded choices. Multiplying these factors gives the answer.
tricks: turn constrained construction into local product factors

solution_variants.name: linear-product-counting
solution_variants.summary: Validate the target sequence, then multiply i+1 on block starts/drops and a_i-(n-i-1) on equal adjacent targets.

tags.evidence: The answer is a product of the number of valid choices contributed by each structural step.
The counted objects are characterized by kth-mex values of all prefixes.
The counting formula is derived from the same constrained constructive characterization as E1.
